free_text: The 34S(alpha,gamma)38Ar, 34S(alpha,n)37Ar and
34S(alpha,p)37Cl cross sections.
Pelletron
A CdS target, enriched to 94.33% in 34S, was prepared
by evaporation from a tantalum boat on to a gold
backing, under vacuum of initial pressure 4*10**(-7)
Torr. The CdS, the boat and the backing were all
outgassed prior to evaporation. The finally adopted
thickness was (393+-33)*10**(15) 34S-atoms cm**(-2).
SD: Year in ref. corrected
Neutron detector which consisted of a 32*32*44
cm3 paraffin castle in which were embedded six
3He-filled proportional counters whose outputs were
connected in parallel. The detection efficiency of the
detector is (10.4+-0.6)% for En=<1 MeV and falls to 7%
at En=4 MeV.
The target was mounted in a stainless-steel tubular
cross located at the centre of the neutron detector.
Statistical errors in the data of figure are smaller
than the plotted points. The uncertainty associated
with the cross-section scale is estimated to be 16%,
made up from detection efficiency 12%, target thickness
10% and beam current integration 3%.
Energy digitizing error
Data digitizing error
Fig.2
A 120 cm3 Ge(Li) detector 2.3 cm from the target
in the 55 degr. direction was used. The detector was
encased in a 2 cm thick lead shield.
Error bars reflect statistical uncertainties.
The uncertainty in the cross-section scale is estimated
to be 10%, made up from target thickness 8%, detection
efficiency 5% and beam current integration 3%.
Energy digitizing error
Data digitizing error
Fig.3
Protons were observed in the 125 degr.
direction by means of a surface-barrier detector
covered with aluminium foil thick enough to stop all
scattered alpha-particles but thin enough to admit the
(a,p0) protons.
A target of natural CdS (95.0% 32S, 4.2% 34S) was used.
Error bars reflect statistical uncertainties.
The uncertainty in the cross-section scale is estimated
to be 12%, made up from target thickness 8%, solid-
angle and beam current integration 7% and angular-
distribution corrections 5%.
